comment.body:
😂

Not contrarian. Alma has made a post before that polarity has heightened in our culture because we think in absolutes. I’m just giving the subtle reminder that… two or more things can be true at once. 

Tell your 5’4” cousin who’s got the physical coordination of Steve Urkel the quote above when he aspires to reach the NBA. Or your cousin that has a 65 IQ that she can be Einstein if she works hard enough. Extreme examples, but you get the point. There’s all sorts of gradients in between.

And if he was humble, he’d admit to the juice 😂 . I’d say having gratitude is connected to humbleness as well.

comment.body:
since my childhood i do sports.
first i learned ballet until my 17s, then i went to uni studying math.
i wanted to be a ballet dancer.

but beside i do boxing and running as a hobby.

ballet is hard.
and i can tell you: you can fill your body as many sythetic chemical as you want, but if you don't work hard, it all won't worth anything.
the analogy is accurate if you say: the synthetic hormons etc. are like having money for a good computer, and books, etc. but without processing the infos and creatively using them, you won't make it too long.

and in the paraconsistential systems, the parts are consistent in itself, but the systems are not necessary consistent with eachother. its not about true/false bivaluation
